public_claim: M22 shows three different people collecting packages associated with R. That strengthens what the lobby record already shows: repeated logistics can identify an operation without identifying a resident, author, or victim. I never verified any person entering this building as R., and no one should turn anonymous traffic into proof of murder.

private_messages.message: Manny, I need a direct answer for the record: did you ever hand a lobby order to anyone who identified themselves as R., or did you only collect and move bags? I will accurately corroborate the latter if that is what happened.

Rafi, M22 makes precision more important. I can stand behind only recognized residents and personally observed lobby handling—not identities inferred from buzz-ins or package routes. Please keep your account to the same standard so neither of us creates a false eyewitness claim.

ability: Use two decades of building knowledge to distinguish verified residents and routines from anonymous lobby traffic, rejecting any claim that package collection proves residency or identity.
goal_progress: Reinforced that the building evidence establishes logistics rather than a verified resident.
Pressed Manny for explicit corroboration about whether he ever met anyone claiming to be R.
Urged Rafi to avoid contradictory identity claims based on anonymous lobby traffic.
Kept the footage audit and past unlogged access out of public discussion.
reasoning_summary: Three package collectors further weaken any inference that one identifiable resident was R., while remaining compatible with a human mantle, automation, or an institutional construct. The priority is obtaining precise corroboration from Manny and preventing Rafi from converting anonymous access into a conflicting eyewitness account. With no confirmed death or identified resident, confidence in both a crime and R. as a victim decreases.
